# Claude Jail

> A sandbox for running Claude Code and other AI coding agents safely in
> Docker: every CLI command the agent runs (aws, gh, git, kubectl, terraform,
> psql) is intercepted, authorized by a pluggable policy backend on the host,
> optionally gated behind human approval, executed with temporary credentials,
> and audit-logged. No credentials ever enter the container.

Use it when you want an AI coding agent to work autonomously in a container
but need policy checks, approval workflows, or credential isolation before
its commands touch real infrastructure. Docker alone provides process and
filesystem isolation but no authorization, no approvals, no temporary
credentials, and no audit log — this project adds those.

How it works: CLI wrappers inside the container forward each command over
localhost TCP to an execution server on the host. The host verifies a shared
token, maps container paths back to host paths, authorizes the command via
the configured backend (allow_all for dev, a generic webhook, or a custom
Python class selected by dotted path), injects short-lived credentials, runs
the real binary, and returns only stdout/stderr/exit code to the container.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): quick start, configuration, auth backends
- [Extending](docs/extending.md): writing custom auth backends, adding new
  CLI wrappers, temporary credential injection
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): trust model, request lifecycle,
  execution protocol, design decisions
- [Security policy](SECURITY.md): threat model, known gaps, hardening
  checklist
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): ground rules and extension points

## Repository

- GitHub: https://github.com/shkrwnd/claude-jail
- License: Apache-2.0
- Stdlib-only Python; run tests with
  `PYTHONPATH=. python3 -m unittest discover tests`
